name: Victoria Hope
school: School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems
focus: Habitat Connectivity & Animal Movement  • Geospatial Analysis & Remote Sensing  • Conservation Planning & Environmental Change
bio: Victoria Hope is a Graduate Research Assistant in the School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems at Northern Arizona University. Her work supports interdisciplinary research, geospatial analysis, and collaborative projects related to remote sensing and applied environmental science. Her research focuses on mapping species distribution, habitat connectivity, and animal movement across large spatial scales, integrating satellite imagery, spatial modeling, and movement data to understand how environmental change and human activity influence wildlife populations. She is particularly interested in bridging technology and conservation to address land-use change and improve human–wildlife interactions, with the goal of supporting data-driven conservation planning and environmental decision-making.
